back_translation: Jesus replied to them in a parable once again, saying:
"The kingdom of heaven is like a king who arranged a royal wedding feast for his royal son.
He sent out his slaves to call those whom he had invited to come to the wedding feast, but they refused to come.

He sent another group of slaves out again, saying, 'Tell those whom I have invited: behold, I have prepared the feast; my oxen and fattened animals have been slaughtered; everything is ready — come to the wedding feast.'
But they ignored it and each went their separate ways — some went to their own fields, some went to tend their own business.
As for the rest, they seized his slaves, some abused them, some killed them.
The king was enraged, so he sent his troops to destroy those murderers and burn their city.
Then he said to his slaves, 'The wedding feast is ready, but those whom I had invited are not worthy.
Therefore, go out to the crossroads along the streets, and whoever you find, invite them to the wedding feast.'
The slaves went out along the streets and gathered everyone they found, both wicked people and good people; and the wedding feast was filled with guests who came to recline at the dining table.
But when the king entered to inspect the guests who had come to recline at the dining table, he saw a man who was not wearing the garment for the wedding feast.
He said to him, 'Friend, how did you enter here without the garment for the wedding feast?' But that man was silent and did not answer.
Then the king commanded the attendants, 'Tie his hands and feet, and throw him out into the outer darkness, where there will be weeping and grinding of teeth.'
For those who are called are many, but those whom He has chosen are few."
After that, the Pharisees went out and together plotted how they might catch Him in His speech.

So they sent their disciples along with the Herodians to meet Him, and said, "Teacher, we know that you are one who speaks the truth, and you teach the way of God in accordance with truth, and you do not defer to anyone, because you do not regard the face of men.
Therefore, please tell us what you think: Is it lawful according to the commandment to pay the poll tax to Caesar or not?"
But Jesus knew their wicked intent, so He said, "You hypocrites, why do you come to test Me?
Show Me the coin used to pay the poll tax." So they brought Him a denarius coin and offered it to Him.
He said to them, "Whose image and inscription is this?"
They replied, "Caesar's." Then He said to them, "Therefore, render the things of Caesar to Caesar, and render the things of God to God."
When they heard this, they were amazed, and left Him and went away.
On that same day, the Sadducees — who say that there is no rising from the dead — came to Him and asked Him,
saying, "Teacher, Moses said, 'If any man dies without having children, his brother must take his wife as his own wife, and beget children to continue the family line for his brother.'
Now, among us there were seven brothers. The eldest married and then died, and since he had no children, his wife passed to his younger brother.
The same thing happened to the second and third brothers, continuing until all seven.
Finally, the woman also died.
Therefore, on the day of rising from the dead, whose wife of the seven will she be? For she had been the wife of all of them."
Jesus answered them, "You are led astray because you do not know the Scriptures and do not know the power of God.
For when rising from the dead, people will not marry and will not be given in marriage, but will be like angels of God in heaven.
But concerning the rising from the dead, have you not read what God has spoken to you, saying,
'I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob'? He is not the God of the dead, but the God of the living."
When the crowd heard this, they were amazed at His teaching.
When the Pharisees heard that He had caused the Sadducees to run out of answers, they gathered together.
Then an expert in the commandment among them asked Him, to test Him, saying,
"Teacher, among all the commandments, which one is the greatest?"
He said to him, "'You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, all your soul, and all your mind.'
This is the great and first commandment.
And the second is the same: 'You shall love your neighbor as you love yourself.'
The whole Law and the words of the prophets each depend on these two commandments."
While the Pharisees were still gathered, Jesus posed a question to them,
saying, "What do you think about the Christ? Whose son is He?" They replied, "The son of David."
He said to them, "If that is the case, why does David, being in the Spirit, call Him 'the Lord,' saying,
'The Lord said to my Lord, Sit at the right hand of Me, until I subdue Your enemies under the soles of Your feet'?
Therefore, if David calls Him 'the Lord' in this way, how can He be the son of David?"
No one was able to answer Him even a single word, and from that day onward, no one dared to question Him any longer.
